deathPlace.address: Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Thomas was born about 1603 in England.

Thomas Canney was sent to Dover New Hampshire by Captain Mason in 1631 or earlier and took a lot of Captain Wiggins in 1634. He had other grants of land in 1652 1656 and after. He was a freeman in 1653 was taxed in 1648 and to 1668 and was alive in 1677. His first wife's name is not known. He married second Jane. Three of his children are mentioned by name in the records:

1.) Thomas

2.) Joseph

3.) Mary

Thomas was one of the signers of The Dover Combination in 1640.

Constable 1648, Grand Jury 1643, 1656; petty 1651. Before 1656 he had bought "Thompson's Point," and was then granted 16 acres adjacent "the outmost point turning up to Cochecho." In 1652 he admimistered Henry Plympton's estate.

In 1652 his eyesight had become impaired, reputation later. He was excused by the court from common training on account of loss of his sight.

In 1671 he had removed to York, but went back to Dover, last mentioned in court for intoxication June 1681.

1633 Captain Wiggin entered upon his duties as Governor and after about one year's residence in the country made a voyage to England. The heads of the principal families now added by Captain Wiggin to the settlement included Thomas Canney. Fresh Creek mill privilege was also granted to Thomas Canney at a rate of six Pounds for the wood besides ten shilling for every such mast as they make use of. The following inhabitants had right of commonage to the ox pasture and calves pasture on Dover Neck where they were land owners though all of them did not live there.

In 1723 his right in the ox pasture, 1-25th of Dover Point, was sold by John (and wife Esther) Hall.

Thomas married twice. His first wife was unknown, thought some refer to her as Jane. She was the mother of Thomas' known children:

1.) Daughter married Matthew Austin

2.) Mary married 1655 Jeremy Tibbetts

3.) Thomas

4.) Hannah born 1641 married Henry Hobbs

5.) Joseph

Thomas was married to second wife Jane (Cannery) by 1652, when she appears in court for beating him. In 1655, Jane was charged with beating Thomas's daughter Mary and her husband.

Thomas Gorges Deputy Gover In ye right of Sir Fardina : Gorges Kt Lord Proprietor of the province of Mayn, given & granted unto Tho : Canny… three Acers of Marsh… between Burchim Poynt & Anthonys Poynt, liing in the Great Marsh that hath been made use of these two yeares by the sd Thomas Canny… paiing unto the sd Sir Fardindo Gorges… one shilling on ye 29 : day of Septembr : Given under my hand this 28 : July 1643 :

Thomas Gorges Depty Govr Thomas then sold this marsh to Hatevil Nutter. Thomas Canny Senjor of… Yorke… in Consideration of Two pounds & ten shillings in hand payd by Mr. Hatevill Nutter of the Town of Dover… do… sell… three Acers of Marsh… in… Kittery Joyneing upon the fore River above Birch point neare the Land of James Emery & Danll Gooding… Comanly Called the fowleing Marsh wch… was granted to mee by Mr Tho : Gorgs… this 16th day of Jany… 1670…

On 5 Dec. 1652 a grant was made to William Furbur, William Wentworth, Henry Langstar and Thomas Canney for "the accommodations of fresh Creeke, for the erecting and settinge up of a sawmill. The bounds of the Timber are from the head of the Creeke from the flowinge of the tide three miles up into the woods betwixt the two freshetts, the southermost freshett cominge out of the Marsh beside the Great Hill at Cochecho, the northermost freshett boundinge Captaine Wiggins & Mr. Bradstreet's grant at Quomphegon, and also what Timber lyes in common that they shall make use of betwixt fresh Creeke and Cochecha: They are to pay six pounds p yeare so longe as they continue possession thereof." Also on the same date the same men were granted "the whole neck of Land from Sentalbons his Cove to the head of fresh Creeke, and so to Cochecha Point: excepting a former Grant made to William Pomfrett. Capt. Richard Walderne is to have and injoy the one halfe of this grant, & the other halfe to the fore mentioned foare men." This was the neck or point formed by the junction of Fresh Creek and the Newichawannock. He has a grant of 16 acres of upland on 6 Dec. 1656 to be laid out adjoining "his perches [purchase] at Tomson's Poynt". This land was laid out from "the outmost point turning up to Cochecho 50 rods to the long creek westward below Tomson's poynt butting on Fore river, thence running three score and ten rods up the long crek side, reserving a cartway from the woods to the water side at the head of the creek, and up Cochecho river three score and ten rods, and thence on a straight line over to the bound at the head of the long creek". "Canney's Creek" or Cove is an inlet from the Pascataqua River on the eastern shore of Newington and was one of the bounds of Ancient Dover being mentioned in 1657 when the lower bounds of Dover were defined as running "from Kenney's Creeke to Hogsty Cove".

"1660 4 mo 26 Presentments ffor Portsmouth Court We Present Thomas Keney for Temtinge Ane Jinckines wife of Ranald Jenckings to unchastity & for urging her all most by fforse both by words & such acctions so unsivell wch I am ashamed to utter : this Shee Testifies to sent hee have an admon & fees of Court."

zh.lifeSummary: Thomas Canney於大約1603年出生在英國肯特多福。他於1632年之前與Mary Canney結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾住了大約8年且於1671年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼約克。他去世於1681年6月之後在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾多福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Thomas Canney于大约1603年出生在英国肯特多福。他于1632年之前与Mary Canney结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾住了大约8年且于1671年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼約克。他去世于1681年6月之后在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾多福。

nameMeanings.name: Canney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Canny
meanings: Irish: either a shortened variant of McCann or of McCanny, which is an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Annaidh ‘son of Annadh’ (a personal name of uncertain meaning). In their Anglicized forms the two surnames were confused.
Irish: from Ó Caithniadh ‘son of Caithnia’, a personal name meaning ‘battle champion’, Anglicized as Caheny or Canny.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."
Historical Boundaries: 1716: York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1776: York, Massachusetts, United States 1820: York, Maine, United States

historicEvents.title: Massachusetts Colony Gains Control of New Hampshire
historicEvents.description: In 1641, Massachusetts gained control of New Hampshire until 1679 when New Hampshire became a separate royal colony.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: First Recorded Slave in Portsmouth
historicEvents.description: In 1645, an African American man taken from Guinea was captured and brought to New Hampshire, he would be counted as the first African American slave in New Hampshire.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Black Heritage Trail in New Hampshire- Our African Heritage

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Canney was born about 1603, in Dover, Kent, England. He married Mary Canney before 1632. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He lived in New Hampshire, British Colonial America for about 8 years and York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America in 1671. He died after June 1681, in Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Canneyは約1603年にイギリスケントドバーで生まれた。1632年以前にMary Canneyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアに約8年間，1671年にはイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーヨークに住んでいた。彼は1681年6月以後にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアドバーで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Canney(은)는 대략 1603년 영국 켄트 도버에서 태어났다. 그는 1632년 이전 Mary Canney(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르에서 약 8년, 1671년도에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 요르크에서 살았다. 그는 1681년 6월 이후 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 도버에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Thomas Canney nasceu aproximadamente 1603, em Dover, Kent, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Mary Canney antes 1632. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele viveu em New Hampshire, British Colonial America por quase 8 anos e em York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America em 1671. Ele faleceu após junho de 1681, em Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas Canney родился около 1603 г. в местности довир, кэнт, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Canney перед 1632 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он жил в местности ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика около 8 лет и в местности ерк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1671 году. Он умер после июнь 1681 г. в местности довир, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Thomas Canney nació aproximadamente 1603, en Dover, Kent, Inglaterra. Se casó con Mary Canney antes de 1632. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Vivió en New Hampshire, British Colonial America durante unos 8 años y en York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America en 1671. Murió después de junio de 1681, en Dover, New Hampshire, British Colonial America.
